title: Python for Beginners
subtitle: Start from zero and build small interactive Python programs
intendedFor: Absolute beginners, students, tutors, and self-learners who want to learn Python fundamentals from the ground up.

courseOverview.recommendedSequence: Complete modules in order. Each module adds one core beginner programming skill and ends with a small project-style outcome.
courseOverview.summary: This course teaches the core foundations of Python: running code, values, variables, input, strings, conditions, and loops.

moduleSummary.title: Getting Started with Python
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Help learners run Python, read simple programs, and build confidence with the basic syntax.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a small personal introduction program that prints formatted information about the learner.

moduleSummary.title: Variables, Input, and Strings
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners how programs remember values, accept user input, and produce readable text.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a simple profile card generator that asks for user details and prints a formatted result.

moduleSummary.title: Conditions and Logic
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Help learners make programs choose different paths using comparisons and conditionals.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a grade or eligibility checker that explains why a result was selected.

moduleSummary.title: Loops and Iteration
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners to repeat actions, process sequences, and control loop behavior.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a mini number game or text analyzer that repeats until a condition is met.

authoringGuidance: This course must always use profileId python and subjectSlug python-v2.
Learners use the ZoeSkoul browser code editor, a Run button, and an output panel.
Do not ask learners to use unavailable local-development workflows such as creating files, installing packages, or running local commands.
Use beginner-safe instructions such as: type code in the editor, click Run, and check the output panel.
Use 'write Python code' instead of 'write a Python script'.
Do not use SQL datasets, SQL tables, sql_query recipes, datasetId, fixedSqlDialect, resultShape, or checkSql in Python lessons.
Python code_input exercises must use recipeType fixed_tests unless the topic explicitly requires semantic checks.
Python fixed_tests exercises must include explicit stdin/stdout tests.
Write code_input solutions as complete runnable programs that read input and print output so the grader can validate them deterministically.
Keep examples beginner friendly: print, values, variables, strings, input, conditions, loops, and simple string operations.

assessmentAndDelivery.suggestedBeginnerRhythm: Two or three short lessons per week, with one small module project after each module.
assessmentAndDelivery.closingNote: Learners finish able to write small interactive Python programs using variables, input, conditions, and loops.

modules.title: Getting Started with Python
modules.purpose: Help learners run Python, read simple programs, and build confidence with the basic syntax.
learningObjectives: Run Python code in a beginner-friendly environment
Explain how statements, expressions, and comments work
Use print output to observe program behavior
modules.moduleProject: Build a small personal introduction program that prints formatted information about the learner.

topics.title: What Python Is Used For
topics.summary: Understand Python's role in automation, web apps, data, beginner programming, and AI.
learningGoals: Identify when Python is a good tool

topics.title: Running Python Code
topics.summary: Use the ZoeSkoul browser code editor, Run button, and output panel to execute Python code.
learningGoals: Type Python code in the browser editor
Click Run and read the output panel

topics.title: Print, Comments, and First Errors
topics.summary: Use print statements and comments while learning how syntax errors appear.

topics.title: Values, Types, and Literals
topics.summary: Recognize strings, integers, floats, booleans, and None.

topics.title: Operators and Expressions
topics.summary: Combine values with arithmetic, comparison, and boolean operators.

topics.title: Reading Error Messages
topics.summary: Learn how to read tracebacks and locate the line that needs attention.

modules.title: Variables, Input, and Strings
modules.purpose: Teach learners how programs remember values, accept user input, and produce readable text.
learningObjectives: Build strings with common methods and f-strings
modules.moduleProject: Build a simple profile card generator that asks for user details and prints a formatted result.

topics.title: Naming and Assigning Variables
topics.summary: Use clear variable names and understand reassignment.

topics.title: Input and Type Conversion
topics.summary: Use input, int, float, and str to handle user-entered values.

topics.title: Common Variable Mistakes
topics.summary: Avoid undefined names, accidental strings, and confusing names.

topics.title: String Indexing and Slicing
topics.summary: Access characters and substrings using indexes and slices.

topics.title: Useful String Methods
topics.summary: Use methods like lower, strip, replace, split, and join.

topics.title: F-Strings and Formatting
topics.summary: Create readable output with f-strings and formatted values.

modules.title: Conditions and Logic
modules.purpose: Help learners make programs choose different paths using comparisons and conditionals.
learningObjectives: Write if, elif, and else blocks
modules.moduleProject: Build a grade or eligibility checker that explains why a result was selected.

topics.title: If, Elif, and Else
topics.summary: Control which code runs based on conditions.

topics.title: Comparisons and Truth Values
topics.summary: Use comparison operators and understand True and False results.

topics.title: Indentation and Blocks
topics.summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

topics.title: And, Or, and Not
topics.summary: Combine conditions with boolean operators.

topics.title: Truthiness and Empty Values
topics.summary: Understand how empty strings, zero, and None behave in conditions.

topics.title: Checking Special Cases First
topics.summary: Use clear early checks to make conditional logic easier to read.
learningGoals: Check simple cases before complex cases

modules.title: Loops and Iteration
modules.purpose: Teach learners to repeat actions, process sequences, and control loop behavior.
learningObjectives: Use for loops and while loops
modules.moduleProject: Build a mini number game or text analyzer that repeats until a condition is met.

topics.title: For Loops Over Text
topics.summary: Loop through a string one character at a time.

topics.title: Range and Counting Patterns
topics.summary: Use range to repeat code a controlled number of times.

topics.title: Accumulators and Counters
topics.summary: Build totals, counts, and running summaries in loops.

topics.title: While Loop Basics
topics.summary: Repeat code until a condition becomes false.

topics.title: Break and Continue
topics.summary: Control loop flow using break and continue.

topics.title: Loop Debugging
topics.summary: Trace loop values to find repeated or skipped work.
